summary: Iteration-3 testing of Meesho Seller Dashboard batch: Accounts CRUD, Labels multi-account run-now, Settings skip rules, daily snapshot, and Alerts. 19/20 new iter3 backend tests pass (1 skipped when no alerts in preview env). Full-suite regression: 39/42 pass — 2 pre-existing iter2 image bugs still failing (HIGH priority, carry-over), 1 iter2 test now outdated because /api/labels/run-now default response shape changed from {job} to {queued,skipped} (expected — update iter2 test). All new UI surfaces verified: AUTOMATION sidebar order (Accounts → Label Download → Settings), Accounts page + Add Account modal pulls defaults (port=9223, profile_dir=/home/ubuntu/chrome-profile2) from /api/accounts/defaults, Labels page has account selector defaulting to 'All enabled accounts (1)' with Account column in Run History, Settings Label Skip Rules with MON-SUN chips + date picker, Bell icon top-right with drawer showing 'Check now' + empty state 'No alerts yet'. FUTURE MODULES correctly cleaned up to only Profit/Loss.

minor.issue: Returns raw serialized job doc (no top-level 'ok' field), while fan-out path returns {ok, queued, skipped} and duplicate path returns {ok, already_queued, id}. Inconsistent shape but clients can still detect success. Consider wrapping single-account create path with {ok: true, job: ...}.

Iter2 test expected the old single-job response; behavior intentionally changed in iter3 (empty body now = all-accounts). Update iter2 test file to assert {queued, skipped} instead of {status:'pending'}.

When running /api/alerts/check-now after /api/scheduler/run-now?what=snapshot back-to-back, the newly-inserted history row has snapshot_at≈now, not 20-30h old, so detect_alerts has no prev snapshot to compare against and returns 0 alerts. Expected in preview env; works as designed after 24h of real history.

ui_bugs.issue: React hydration warning in console: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>'. Caused by Emergent visual-editor wrapping the dynamic text (enabledAccounts.length) inside <span style='display:contents'> inside <option> at LabelsPage.js:101. Not breaking but floods the console and is a hydration error. Fix: build the option label as a pre-concatenated string (e.g. `All enabled accounts (${n})`) without any inline JSX expression separators — or mark the dynamic span with x-excluded true pass-through.

action_items: Carry-over HIGH from iter2: /api/products list image fallback to review media (still returns image=null for aeop5q/8sa1ay).
Carry-over HIGH from iter2: ProductDetailPage hero must bind to product.image (not product.product_image_large_url).
LOW: Fix LabelsPage <option> hydration warning by flattening dynamic label into plain string.
LOW: Update iter2 test_run_now_and_idempotent to match new fan-out shape ({queued, skipped}).
LOW (nice-to-have): make single-account /api/labels/run-now response consistent with fan-out by wrapping in {ok, job}.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:715 create_account relies on name+port uniqueness application-side, but unique indexes on db.accounts (name, debug_port) are also enforced at startup — duplicate index errors would return as 400 via the application check which is correct. Good.
enqueue_daily_label_job respects is_skipped_today() — verified: setting today's IST date in skip_dates + POST /api/scheduler/run-now?what=label creates 0 new scheduler-submitted jobs. Correct.
detect_alerts dedup_key uses YYYY-MM-DD (UTC) — means an alert raised at 23:59 UTC vs 00:01 UTC next day is treated as different; fine for this use-case.
snapshot_all_products writes one row per tracked product per call — verified by diffing /api/products/8sa1ay/history?days=1 before/after.
label_run_now fan-out correctly skips accounts with an existing pending/processing job per-account (idempotent).
alerts dedup_key unique-sparse index created at startup — prevents duplicate alert inserts within same day. Good.
Minor: /api/settings PUT re-fetches via get_settings(user) which couples helper to auth dep — works but not ideal (carried over from iter2).
Minor: No rate limit on POST /api/alerts/check-now (admin-only endpoint, acceptable).

success_rate.backend: 95% (19/20 new iter3 + 39/42 full regression; 2 fails are known iter2 bugs, 1 is outdated iter2 test)
success_rate.frontend: 98% (all new UI elements render and work; 1 low-priority console hydration warning on LabelsPage option)

seed_data_creation: Created several accounts prefixed 'qa_' (all deleted via fixture teardown); created transient label_download pending jobs (remain in db because no worker in preview — expected). Briefly set skip_dates to today's IST date and then restored skip_dates=[] + skip_weekdays=[] in teardown. Existing 'Main' account (port 9222) preserved. Two tracked products aeop5q & 8sa1ay untouched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration3.py -v`. Full suite: `REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. The restore_settings fixture restores skip_dates/skip_weekdays after the suite. The test_mark_read_and_delete test is auto-skipped when no alerts exist (preview has no 24h history). To manually create an alert for UI-badge testing, insert a doc directly into db.alerts with {read:false, created_at:now, message:'...', dedup_key: uuid} — there is no public POST /api/alerts endpoint. The two iter2 image-fallback bugs remain unfixed and cause known failures in test_iteration2.py — those are HIGH priority but carry-over (main agent will address).
rca_of_the_issue: Hydration warning RCA: LabelsPage.js line 101 renders `<option value='__all__'>All enabled accounts ({enabledAccounts.length})</option>`. The Emergent visual-editor transform wraps the `{enabledAccounts.length}` expression in an inline `<span style={{display:'contents'}}>`, which React correctly rejects inside `<option>` (option children must be plain text). Fix: change JSX to `<option value='__all__'>{`All enabled accounts (${enabledAccounts.length})`}</option>` so the entire child becomes one interpolated string — no embedded span needed. No functional impact; selector still works and submits correctly.
